title: athlete — published events
description: Every event implements `ShouldDispatchAfterCommit` so listeners never fire against a rolled-back write. Athlete lifecycle events (create / status-change / pause / graduate / withdraw / archive / restore) fan out to notifications + activity + audit. AthleteMedicalUpdated is a PRIVATE-TIER event — dispatches on the dedicated `medical-audit` queue and the payload NEVER routes through the tenant activity feed (the audit row lands in the separate athlete_medical_history table). AthleteMinorTurnedAdult fires from the CheckAthleteMinorTurnedAdultJob on the 18th birthday for compliance-visibility.

events.name: AthleteCreated
events.trigger: AthleteProvisioner committed successfully. Fires exactly once per athlete row. Consumes 1 unit of athlete_slot before commit (observer creating hook).
consumers: notifications::DispatchAthleteWelcomeNotification (to the created-by admin AND to any primary guardian resolved at create-time — Wave 3b hooks the guardian path in the same commit as athlete-guardian)
athlete::SetAgeGroupSnapshotOnAthleteCreated (in-band snapshot resolution — the row is unusable in enrollment forms without a snapshot)

events.name: AthleteUpdated
events.trigger: Any Athlete scalar field mutated except status (dedicated transition events), consent_* (AthleteConsentRecorded / AthleteConsentRevoked / AthletePhotoConsentChanged), medical_* (AthleteMedicalUpdated — private-tier), emergency_contact_* (AthleteEmergencyContactUpdated), user_id (AthleteLinkedToUser), current_age_group_id (AthleteAgeGroupSnapshotUpdated).

events.name: AthleteLinkedToUser
events.trigger: Athlete.user_id transitioned null → non-null. Fires when the minor athlete turns 13-16 + gets a User account, OR when an adult signup's Athlete row is bound to the newly-created User. Never fires for user_id changes (immutable post-first-link).
consumers: notifications::DispatchAthleteLinkedToUserNotification (to the User's login channel — welcome to your athlete account)

events.name: AthleteStatusChanged
events.trigger: Athlete.status transitioned. Umbrella event; also fires alongside the dedicated status events (AthletePausedForSafeguarding / AthleteGraduated / AthleteWithdrawn / AthleteArchived / AthleteRestored) so listeners can subscribe to the umbrella when the specific reason doesn't matter to them.

events.name: AthleteAgeGroupSnapshotUpdated
events.trigger: RollAthleteAgeGroupSnapshotJob detected the athlete's resolved age-group flipped (birthday drift + AgeGroupBoundsChanged / AgeGroupCutoffChanged cascade). Aggregated event — fires once per job invocation per athlete whose snapshot changed. Athlete.current_age_group_id + current_age_group_snapshot_at are updated on the same transaction.
consumers: notifications::DispatchAthleteAgeGroupSnapshotChangedNotification (opt-in — to guardians when the child moves to a new age group, e.g. U10 → U12)
athlete-enrollment::MaybeInvalidateEnrollmentEligibility (Wave 3+ — enrollments whose team's age_group_id no longer matches this athlete's snapshot fire a soft-warning flag)

events.name: AthleteMedicalUpdated
events.trigger: Any of medical_conditions / medical_allergies / medical_medications / medical_notes mutated. PRIVATE-TIER event — payload NEVER routed through the activity feed. Audit row lands in the dedicated athlete_medical_history table with 7-year regulated-health retention. Consumers include the Safeguarding module (Wave 4+ medical-clearance workflow).
consumers: audit::WriteToMedicalHistoryLog (retention: 7 years; NOT the normal audits table)
notifications::DispatchAthleteMedicalUpdatedNotification (opt-in — to primary AthleteGuardian ONLY, via HR channel)

events.name: AthleteConsentRecorded
events.trigger: Athlete.consent_recorded_at transitioned null → non-null OR was refreshed with new values (re-consent). Fires alongside AthletePhotoConsentChanged when photo consent specifically flipped.

payload.consent_recorded_by_user_id: ULID (usually the primary AthleteGuardian for minors)
payload.expires_at: ISO 8601 | null (12 months from recorded_at when athlete_consent_workflow entitlement is on)

events.name: AthleteConsentRevoked
events.trigger: Any consent_* flag transitioned true → false. Consent revocation is a compliance-critical event. Fires per revocation (multiple flags flipping in one write emits multiple events for auditability).

events.name: AthletePausedForSafeguarding
events.trigger: Athlete.status transitioned active → paused. Distinct from the umbrella AthleteStatusChanged because pause is safeguarding-adjacent and drives dedicated notification cascade + auto-expiry job.

events.name: AthleteReturnedFromPause
events.trigger: Athlete.status transitioned paused → active. Clears paused_at + paused_reason.

events.name: AthleteGraduated
events.trigger: Athlete.status transitioned active → graduated. Soft-terminal — the row is retained for alumni relations.

events.name: AthleteWithdrawn
events.trigger: Athlete.status transitioned active/paused → withdrawn. Terminal. Requires withdrawal_reason.
consumers: notifications::DispatchAthleteWithdrawnNotification (to admin + coach; guardian is opt-in — some withdrawals are guardian-initiated)

events.name: AthleteArchived
events.trigger: Athlete soft-deleted (deleted_at set). Status transitions to 'archived'. Releases 1 unit of athlete_slot entitlement.

events.name: AthleteRestored
events.trigger: Archived athlete restored (deleted_at cleared, status → active). Consumes 1 unit of athlete_slot. Only within retention window.

events.name: AthleteDeleted
events.trigger: Athlete hard-deleted. Fires from PurgeArchivedAthletesJob after 7-year retention window + no downstream references. Also fires on TenantErased cascade.

events.name: AthleteEmergencyContactUpdated
events.trigger: Any of emergency_contact_name / emergency_contact_phone / emergency_contact_relationship mutated. Audit-relevant for safety compliance.
consumers: activity::WriteToActivityLog (WITHOUT the field values — activity feed shows 'emergency contact updated', not the phone number)

events.name: AthletePhotoConsentChanged
events.trigger: Athlete.consent_photo_release specifically flipped. Distinct event because it drives immediate downstream cascade (marketing collateral, gallery visibility, social-media auto-inclusion — Wave 5+).
consumers: notifications::DispatchAthletePhotoConsentChangedNotification (to marketing team — cannot-opt-out; ensures marketing pauses use)

events.name: AthleteMinorTurnedAdult
events.trigger: CheckAthleteMinorTurnedAdultJob detected an athlete crossing config.athlete.minor_min_age (default 16) OR 18 (legal-adult boundary). Fires per athlete per crossing. Downstream flags: consent snapshot should be re-recorded by the athlete themselves (their prior consents by a guardian expire); AthleteGuardian rows with can_authorise_medical_care=true may auto-downgrade (config-driven, Wave 3b).
consumers: notifications::DispatchAthleteMinorTurnedAdultNotification (to athlete + primary guardian — cannot-opt-out; drives the re-consent flow)

payload.consent_re_record_required: boolean (true when boundary_crossed='legal_adult_18' + prior consents were guardian-recorded)
